Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for semiconductors, networking, and related hardware. Software adoption is also growing, though some areas face valuation scrutiny.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — would disrupt supply chain
Significant slowdown in enterprise software spending growth below 10% YoY

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Retail is facing mixed signals with some segments showing resilience while others grapple with operational challenges and shifting consumer behavior. Residential construction shows resilience, but auto manufacturers face autonomy hurdles.
watchFor: Consumer spending data shows significant contraction
Interest rates rise further, impacting housing demand
Major retail earnings miss expectations across the board

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and entertainment sectors are experiencing varied performance, with some companies showing strong growth and innovation while others face competitive pressures and valuation concerns.
watchFor: Significant decline in digital advertising spending
Major streaming service subscriber losses accelerate
New regulatory pressures on social media platforms

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense is seeing robust demand and strong backlogs, while specialty industrial machinery is benefiting from new contracts and innovation.
watchFor: Significant cuts in global defense spending
Major supply chain disruptions for industrial components
Economic slowdown impacts heavy machinery demand

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug market continues to drive strong performance for leaders, while other healthcare segments show stable but less dynamic growth.
watchFor: New competitive GLP-1 drugs enter the market with superior efficacy/safety
Regulatory setbacks for key GLP-1 drugs
PBMs significantly reduce GLP-1 coverage or reimbursement rates

Financials.thesis: Major banks and credit services companies are showing resilience with strong Q1 results and positive analyst sentiment, despite some individual stock volatility.
watchFor: Unexpected rise in loan defaults
Significant slowdown in consumer spending impacting credit services
Fed signals prolonged higher interest rates, impacting bank profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The consumer defensive sector is experiencing mixed signals, with some companies showing momentum while others face operational challenges and shifting consumer behavior.
watchFor: Consumer spending shifts dramatically away from staples
Inflationary pressures significantly erode profit margins
Major supply chain disruptions impact product availability

Utilities.thesis: Independent power producers are showing strong performance, driven by positive analyst sentiment and favorable market conditions.
watchFor: Significant increase in natural gas prices
Regulatory changes unfavorable to independent power producers
Economic slowdown reduces industrial power demand

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is facing mixed signals, with some companies experiencing downward pressure despite strategic partnerships and strong fundamentals.
watchFor: Significant decline in global industrial production
Increased trade tensions impacting raw material flows
New supply sources disrupt commodity prices

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is gaining traction as a safe-haven asset amid geopolitical hopes, while silver's performance is mixed due to tax and fee concerns.
watchFor: Significant strengthening of the US dollar
Real yields rise sharply
Geopolitical tensions de-escalate unexpectedly
